methods.method_name: Statistical Analysis of Pollen Allergy Data
methods.description: This method involves statistical procedures to analyze the relationship between meteorological factors, tree pollen counts, and the number of patients with tree pollen allergy. It includes correlation analysis, logistic regression, and adjustments for confounding variables.

steps.description: Consider lag times between meteorological factors, tree pollen, and patients with tree pollen allergy to satisfy appropriate temporal relationships.
steps.searchable_summary: align data based on lag times between meteorological factors and pollen allergy cases

steps.description: Analyze differences in age and sex in patients with and without tree pollen allergy using the independent t-test and chi-square test.
steps.searchable_summary: perform t-test and chi-square test on patient demographics

steps.description: Use Pearson's correlation analysis to investigate correlations between meteorological factors, tree pollen counts, and the number of patients with tree pollen allergy.
Calculate odds ratios (ORs) of sensitization to tree pollen according to variations in meteorological factors using logistic regression analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: perform logistic regression to calculate odds ratios

steps.description: Adjust for air pollutants, age, and sex in the regression analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: include confounding variables in regression model

steps.description: Perform statistical analyses using SPSS version 17.0 software, and consider a p-value of < 0.05 as significant.
steps.searchable_summary: use SPSS for statistical analysis with significance threshold

methods.description: This method describes the criteria and process for selecting study subjects from patients with allergic symptoms referred to Ajou University Hospital.

steps.description: Sample patients who had allergic symptoms and were referred from local clinics to Ajou University Hospital between 1999 and 2008.
steps.searchable_summary: select patient records from hospital database

steps.description: Select patients who performed skin prick tests to identify causative allergens and review their medical records.
steps.searchable_summary: filter patients with skin prick test results

steps.description: Use patients aged 18 years or older tested from April to July as study subjects.
steps.searchable_summary: filter patients by age and test date

steps.description: Exclude patients without a response to a positive control or with a response to a negative control.
steps.searchable_summary: exclude invalid skin prick test results

steps.description: Exclude patients in 2001 due to missing skin prick test results.
